bays.name: Bay 1 — Audio Accessories WDBP-9615-SH (Bantam/TT)
bays.description: Main patchbay. Top row is outputs, bottom row is inputs. Preamp outputs are normaled to A/D inputs.

sections.label: Preamp OUTPUTS
sections.detail: N72 (1-4), A12 (5-6), Pacifica A/B (7-8), AEA RPQ A/B (9-10), Sebatron (11-12), Sytek (13-16)

sections.label: Outboard OUTPUTS
sections.detail: FMR RNC (1-2), Ensoniq DP/4 (1-4), DBX 560A, Lindell PEX (1-2), FET 500 (1-2)

sections.label: MH Line INPUTS — LiO-1
sections.detail: Metric Halo LiO-1 inputs 1-8. Normaled from preamp outputs 1-8.

sections.label: MH Line INPUTS — LiO-2
sections.detail: Metric Halo LiO-2 inputs 1-8. Normaled from preamp outputs 9-16.

sections.label: Outboard INPUTS
sections.detail: Vermite MkC-1, FMR RNC (1-2), Ensoniq DP/4 (1-4), DBX 560A, Lindell PEX (1-2), FET 500 (1-2)

bays.normaling: Half-normaled: preamp outputs (top 1-16) are normaled to LiO A/D inputs (bottom 1-16). Inserting a cable in the bottom row breaks the normal and lets you route to a different destination.

bays.name: Bay 2 — Black Lion Audio PBR TRS3 (1/4" TRS Jack)
bays.description: Line-level TRS patchbay for synths, guest inputs, and outboard sends/returns.

sections.label: Guest 1-4
sections.detail: Guest input jacks for visiting gear

bays.name: Bay 3 — ART P16 Termination Panel
bays.description: 16-point termination panel. C84 preamps, Verville, and Lola 500-series connections.

bays.name: Bay 4 — ART P16 Termination Panel
bays.description: 16-point termination panel mirroring the preamp layout. Direct preamp I/O access.

sections.label: Channels 1-16
sections.detail: Preamp termination points matching top row

tips: Bay 1 is the main signal routing hub. Preamp outputs on top are normaled to A/D inputs on bottom. No patching needed for basic recording.
To bypass a preamp and go straight to A/D: plug your source into the bottom row of Bay 1, breaking the normal.
To insert outboard: take the preamp output from Bay 1 top row, route to the outboard input, then route the outboard output back to the A/D input on Bay 1 bottom row.
Bay 2 is for line-level instruments (synths, drum machines). Top row = outputs FROM the instruments, bottom row = inputs TO the instruments.
Bay 3 and 4 are termination panels that give you direct access to specific preamp and module I/O.
Bantam (TT) cables for Bay 1. Standard 1/4" TRS cables for Bays 2, 3, and 4.
Push patch cables in firmly. A loose connection causes intermittent signal or no signal.
